---
title: "Quest 6: Functions - Reusable Steps"
subtitle: "Write Functions with Parameters and Returns"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
✨ QUEST 6 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 8 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [View Notebook](../files/lessons/06-functions.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/06-functions.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Functions make code cleaner and reusable.

## 💡 Function Shape

```rust
fn greet(name: &str) {
    println!("Hello, {}!", name);
}

fn main() {
    greet("Riley");
}
```

Functions can return values:

```rust
fn double(n: i32) -> i32 {
    n * 2
}

fn main() {
    println!("{}", double(7));
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Write `add(a, b)` that returns the sum and print the result for `3` and `9`.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn add(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32 {
    a + b
}

fn main() {
    let result = add(3, 9);
    println!("Sum: {}", result);
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 6 Complete
You used parameters, return types, and function calls.
Next: [Quest 7: HashMaps](07-dictionaries.qmd)
:::
